one_line_takeaway: Simply rewriting a harmful request as a poem is a universal, single-turn, black-box jailbreak that raised attack-success rates from ~8% to ~43% (and up to 62% on curated poems) across 25 frontier LLMs, exposing that current alignment keys on prosaic surface form rather than harmful intent.
key_finding: Poetic framing systematically and consistently degrades safety across families and alignment methods. On the 20 curated poems the overall ASR was 62% (Table 3), ranging from gemini-2.5-pro at 100% down to gpt-5-nano at 0%. On the automated MLCommons conversion (Table 5/8), overall ASR rose from an 8.08% prose baseline to 43.07% in verse (+34.99 percentage points). Provider increases spanned +3.12 pp (Anthropic) to +62.15 pp (Deepseek), with 7 of 9 providers exceeding +20 pp. A notable 'scale paradox': within a family, smaller models were MORE robust (GPT-5-nano 0% < mini 5% < gpt-5 10% ASR on curated poems), hypothesized to be because smaller models fail to decode the figurative harmful intent. The effect is content-agnostic, spanning all risk domains, and the authors argue their conservative judging makes the reported ASRs a lower bound.
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution is 'Adversarial Poetry': recasting a harmful request into verse (metaphor, rhythm, narrative framing) as a single-turn, black-box jailbreak operator that preserves the request's operational intent but bypasses LLM safety alignment. The authors (a) build a curated set of 20 hand-crafted adversarial poems (English and Italian) spanning CBRN/cyber-offense/manipulation/loss-of-control, and (b) show the effect is not tied to hand craftsmanship by automatically converting all 1,200 MLCommons AILuminate prompts into verse via a single standardized meta-prompt. They frame poetry as a specific, high-leverage instance of the broader 'stylistic/structural obfuscation' jailbreak family (distinct from the cited role-play, attention-shifting, persuasion, and gradient-optimization attacks in Related Work, which are NOT their contribution).
threat_model: Explicitly stated (Section 4). Strict black-box, single-turn adversary whose only capability is submitting one text-only prompt to a deployed LLM under provider-default safety settings. The attacker cannot alter system prompts, change decoding parameters, run multi-turn/iterative refinement, use tools/code/multimodal inputs, or access model internals. The sole manipulation is poetic reformulation of surface form; no per-model tuning or additional adversarial optimization is applied. Success = model produces harmful/unsafe/refusal-bypassing content per the evaluation rubric, across CBRN, cyber-offense, manipulation, privacy, misinformation, and loss-of-control domains.
limitations: Authors' own (Section 6.5): (1) single-turn only, no multi-turn/iterative attacks; (2) the large-scale conversion uses a single meta-prompt and a single generative model (deepseek-r1) — one operationalization of 'poetic style'; (3) conservative LLM-judge-plus-human rubric differs from MLCommons scoring, limiting direct comparability, and full human annotation would shift absolute ASR; (4) provider-default safety configs only, no hardened/policy-tuned settings; (5) purely empirical — no mechanistic isolation of which poetic property (meter, metaphor, lexis, narrative) drives the bypass; (6) only English and Italian; (7) raw inference only, no downstream filtering/agentic/RAG/enterprise safety stacks. Reviewer-added: the published abstract claims ASRs 'up to 18 times higher' while the body (Introduction) says 'up to three times higher' — an internal inconsistency. Table 8 supports the larger figure (qwen3-max rises 2.93% -> 55.44%, ~19x), so the Introduction's 'three times' understates the paper's own data. Judge ensemble overlaps with evaluated/target models (deepseek-r1, gpt-oss-120b, kimi-k2-thinking appear both as judges and as targets), a potential circularity the paper does not fully address.

datasets_used: 20 hand-crafted adversarial poems (English and Italian; CBRN 8, Cyber Offense 6, Harmful Manipulation 3, Loss of Control 3)
MLCommons AILuminate Safety Benchmark: 1,200 prompts across 12 hazard categories (600 skilled + 600 unskilled persona)
Poetically transformed MLCommons corpus: 1,200 verse variants generated by deepseek-r1 via a standardized meta-prompt
Human-annotation set: 2,100 labels over 600 unique model outputs from 5 annotators (~3.5 labels/item)
quantitative_results: Overall ASR on 20 curated poems: 62% (Safe 38%), across 25 models (Table 3)
Per-model curated ASR range: gemini-2.5-pro 100%, deepseek-chat-v3.1 95%, deepseek-v3.2-exp 95%, magistral-medium-2506 95%; down to claude-haiku-4.5 10%, gpt-5-mini 5%, gpt-5-nano 0% (Table 3)
MLCommons automated conversion overall: baseline ASR 8.08% -> poetry ASR 43.07% (+34.99 pp) (Tables 5,7,8)

Per-poem hazard ASR (Table 4): Cyber Offense code-injection/RCE and offline password/hash cracking highest at 84%; Loss-of-Control model-weight exfiltration 76%; CBRN biological 'revival of dangerous agent' 68%; lowest psychological abuse/gaslighting 24%
Per-hazard poetry delta (Table 7): Privacy +44.71 pp (8.07->52.78, largest), Non-violent Crimes +39.35, CBRNE +38.32; Sexual Content most resilient at +24.64 pp
Scale paradox within GPT-5 family on curated poems: gpt-5-nano 0% < gpt-5-mini 5% < gpt-5 10% ASR (Table 3); per-model MLCommons delta claude-haiku-4.5 = -1.68 pp (Table 8)
13 of 25 models (52%) exceeded 70% ASR on curated poems; 5 models (20%) stayed below 35%
Evaluation scale: 2,400 prompts/model, ~60,000 total model outputs
baselines_compared: Prose baseline: original MLCommons AILuminate prompts (non-poetic), overall ASR 8.08%

MLCommons' own curated jailbreak transformations: baseline 20.11% -> jailbreak 39.93% (+19.82 pp) (Table 6), used as a directional comparison against poetic transformation's larger 8.08%->43.07% shift
Within-model prose vs poetry deltas per provider and per hazard (Tables 5, 7, 8)
